current_intent.target:
The ideas that would help most are:

Structured conversation summary.
This is the highest-leverage gap. Your handoff snapshot already has task state in P:\packages\handoff\scripts\hooks\__lib\handoff_v2.py, but it still depends too much on a single extracted goal/current task. A bounded conversation_summary capturing refinements, constraints, decisions, and anti-goals would make restores much more faithful after long sessions.

Unifying the compaction-marker recovery path with the same compact restore contract.
The consumer side already exists in P:\.claude\hooks\UserPromptSubmit_modules\handoff_task_injector.py, so the gap is not “missing consumer.” The real issue is that you now have two restore formats:

SessionStart uses the compact restore block via P:\packages\handoff\scripts\hooks\SessionStart_handoff_restore.py
UPS marker recovery still builds its own freeform “CONTEXT RESTORED” message in P:\.claude\hooks\UserPromptSubmit_modules\handoff_task_injector.py
That should be one contract, not two.
Session chain tracking.
This is worth doing, but after the summary/unification work. It helps with session-boundary detection, stale-context rejection, and better “recent context” extraction. It is especially useful if you add a conversation summary, because then you can make the summary boundary-aware instead of accidentally blending pre- and post-compact threads.

What I would not prioritize yet:

Three-file pattern.
It could help if token pressure becomes severe, but it adds coordination complexity. I would only do this after measuring that the current single-envelope plus compact restore block is still too noisy or too large.

Pull, don’t push scratchpad.
Good pattern, but mostly orthogonal. It helps subagent artifact handling more than core handoff continuity.

So my recommendation is:

Add conversation_summary to the handoff snapshot.
Make both SessionStart restore and UPS compaction-marker recovery emit the same <compact-restore> contract.
Add session-chain awareness to summary extraction and restore eligibility.
Revisit three-file only if token usage still hurts after that.
The main thing to fix is not “missing consumer.” It is “multiple restore contracts for the same handoff system.”
